reasoning: The question asks if the US will collect between $500 billion and $1 trillion in customs duties in FY 2025. FY 2025 runs from October 1, 2024, to September 30, 2025. In FY 2024, the US collected around $82.2 billion. Achieving over $500 billion in FY 2025 would require an extraordinary increase in tariffs. While President-elect Trump has proposed sweeping tariffs (e.g., 10-20% universal, 60% on China), he does not take office until January 20, 2025, meaning nearly a third of the fiscal year will have elapsed under existing tariff structures. For the remaining 8 months of FY 2025, new tariffs would need to be implemented almost immediately and generate revenue at an unprecedented annualized rate of roughly $700 billion+ without causing a proportional immediate collapse in import volumes. Given the statutory and administrative delays in enacting tariffs (such as Section 301 investigations or IEEPA declarations), potential legal injunctions, exemptions, and the short window remaining in the fiscal year, it is virtually impossible that customs receipts will sextuple to eclipse the $500 billion mark within FY 2025. Thus, the probability of reaching this interval is extremely low.
